markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Write the spec-ready contract for local/cloud model lane attribution, leases, receipts, checkpoints, and restart recovery. This MT belongs to MasterSpecEnrichmentSeed. Prepare the future Master Spec refinement/enrichment payload without relying on sidecar research.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Prepare spec-enrichment-ready wording without editing the Master Spec in this stub task.
Where current Master Spec text is stale, mark the exact stale contract and the replacement Handshake-native rule.
scope.expected_behavior: SwarmRecoverySpecSeed leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: SwarmRecoverySpecSeed is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

AgentLaneLease: An agent lane lease MUST be claimed before a model session mutates ProjectKnowledgeIndex drafts, rich-document drafts, index runs, or graph mutation proposals.

A lease record MUST carry: lease_id (ULID), lane_id, actor_id, actor_kind (operator|local_model|cloud_model|validator|system), session_id, correlation_id, scope_ref (workspace|document|source_root|index_run), claimed_at_utc, expires_at_utc, renewal_count, released_at_utc (nullable), takeover_of (nullable).
Leases MUST expire and expiry MUST be enforced server-side; a write under an expired or foreign lease MUST be denied with a typed denial receipt.
Takeover: a new actor MAY claim an expired lease's scope; the takeover lease MUST record takeover_of = prior lease_id and MUST emit a lease-takeover event.
Renewal MUST extend expires_at_utc without changing lease identity and MUST emit a renewal event.
All lease transitions (claim/renew/expire/release/takeover/denial) MUST be EventLedger events with idempotency keys.
SwarmCheckpoint: A swarm checkpoint MUST capture: checkpoint_id, session_id, actor_id, lane_id, lease_id, scope_ref, resume_pointer (typed: MT id, claim id, document revision, or index-run position), created_at_utc, payload_hash.
Checkpoints MUST be durable PostgreSQL/EventLedger records; recovery after session loss or compaction MUST be reconstructable from checkpoints plus receipts alone, with no chat-history dependency.
A recovery MUST emit a recovery receipt linking the new session to the recovered checkpoint and lease lineage.

swarm_lease_checkpoint_contract_seed.application_status: FUTURE_SPEC_BUMP_MATERIAL_ONLY - not applied to v02.192; today's applied law is limited to 02#2.3.13.11 backend-navigation receipt requirements (actor/session/correlation ids).

evidence: Spec-ready lease/checkpoint contract drafted as scope.swarm_lease_checkpoint_contract_seed in this MT contract (MUST-level claim/expiry/renewal/takeover/denial semantics + durable checkpoint/recovery records).
Seed aligned with implementation owner MTs MT-209/210/213/214/216 so the external validator can diff implementation against the seed.
Attribution/receipt base law already applied in v02.192 02#2.3.13.11 (backend navigation receipts with actor/session/correlation ids).

AC3 (no sidecar authority): the seed is typed contract content inside this MT contract, derived from stub state_recovery_contract + PRODUCT-AC-008, not from external papers; research provenance is non-normative per stub research_basis.
AC4 (HBR rows): hbr_focus HBR-SWARM-001/002/003 are implementation obligations transferred to owner MTs (now MT-209/210/213/214/215/216/217/223); seed itself is record-scope, NA per HBRNA-002.

concerns: Contract freshness skew: scope.relevant_files and the activation constraint pin the v02.191 bundle while SPEC_CURRENT and all evidence are v02.192 (contracts hydrated 07:20Z after the 06:45Z spec bump); validator should read the constraint intent (indexed bundle, not v02.182 monolith), but a hygiene pass updating contract pins to v02.192 would remove the ambiguity.
AC coverage: evidence addresses the seed deliverable only; AC3 (sidecar rejection) and AC4 (HBR disposition - hbr_focus includes CX-503R) have no evidence entries, unlike sibling MTs MT-034..048.

Owner-MT list incomplete vs scope: scope includes lane attribution but MT-215 (LocalCloudAttribution) is absent from swarm_lease_checkpoint_contract_seed.implementation_owner_mts (MT-209/210/213/214/216); MT-217 (parallel overwrite safety) and MT-223 (stale-lease negative cases) also implement seed semantics (denial, expiry proof) and are unlisted, so a validator diffing implementation against the seed via the listed owners alone would miss surfaces.

Applied-vs-seeded clarity: the lease/checkpoint contract is NOT in v02.192 (status SEED_READY_FOR_FUTURE_SPEC_BUMP, consistent with the no-spec-edit constraint); only the attribution/receipt base law of EV3 is applied law (02#2.3.13.11). EV wording is honest but a validator must not read EV1 as applied spec law.
adversarial_review.refutation_attempts: Opened every cited artifact (refinement.json, spec-enrichment-proposal.json, stub contract, v02.192 spec modules 02/07/10/11/12, spec-changelog.jsonl, SPEC_CURRENT.md, packet.json); attacked criterion coverage AC1-AC4, citation validity, specificity, v02.192 freshness, and scope-artifact existence. Verified the seed contains MUST-level claim/expiry(server-enforced)/renewal/takeover(takeover_of lineage)/denial(typed receipt) lease semantics and durable checkpoint/recovery records with no-chat-history reconstruction; read MT-209/210/213/214/216 scopes and confirmed each maps to seed semantics; confirmed 02#2.3.13.11 receipt law. Fix: add MT-215 (and consider MT-217/223) to implementation_owner_mts and add AC3/AC4 evidence entries.

items: Added MT-215 (LocalCloudAttribution), MT-217 (ParallelEditorAgentSafety), MT-223 (StateRecoveryNegativeCases) to implementation_owner_mts.
Marked seed explicitly as future-spec-bump material with today's applied law boundary.

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
